item.title: Operational Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Please read the entirety of this post to understand why the image has been removed despite the exchange above.

Current image is owned by andre govia.

While the author has indeed obtained permission from the original creator to use the image, they misrepresented the SCP-112 in their original email as a non-commercial piece of work. The SCP Wiki is under a CC BY-SA 3.0 license, which is a commercial license by default. This is most prominent in how we disallow usage of non-commercial images: it is why SCP merchandise can be made in the first place.

This raises the question of whether the original image owner was fully aware of the site's license. As such, Licensing Team has decided that the image will be removed until further notice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This should be renamed "Mr Bones Wild Ride"

+1
I didn't realize I hadn't upvoted this til now. Congrats MisterBibs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deaugh, roller coasters, my one true fear. So, for that I applaud you. And also hate you. But mostly applaud you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I could think during this whole article was…the ride never ends

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ability to upvote this article was one of the reasons I decided to become a member.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That D-Class was writing. They didn't transcribe incoherent ramblings. What the person wrote was added to the file. If there was strikethrough text, it's because the person wrote something, then crossed it out. Kinda hard to represent writing properly in typed text, but that's what was going on there.

And I think the first interview was a female D-Class. The second was a male.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea, even as one who loves rollercoasters… the idea of being stuck on one for months at a time is just… *shudders*.
In my opinion, everything in this article is near perfect, except for the final interview log. Seriously, on a formal document, the Foundation wouldn't write

screamed. screamed over and over. girl wouldn't answer me.

Just because the girl is talking in a desperate, incoherent fashion doesn't remove the need for punctuation and capitalization. That being said, the last two logs added much more than they detracted from the article, so that didn't affect my upvote.

item.title: Re: Permission Attained (1 of 2)
item.content:encoded: That's fine. Thank you for getting permission and showing proof.

item.title: Permission Attained (1 of 2)
item.content:encoded:
The first picture used in this story has been given approval by the artist, Andre Govia.

http://i.imgur.com/KEoFrq6.jpg

Original source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/andregovia/5226860063/

I'll make a minor edit to put back the first picture.

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded: Once you have permission from the owners, the pictures will go back up.

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded: I said I contacted them (maybe 6 hours ago). If and when I get a response, I'd of course post it either way.

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded: I mean their replies.

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded:
Proof that I sent out a "hey, can I use this?" mail? Ookay..

http://i.imgur.com/67e3sJC.jpg

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded: Can you provide proof of that?

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded:
https://www.flickr.com/photos/jensofjapan/5032964365/in/faves-10496336@N05/
Copyright 2006

https://www.flickr.com/photos/andregovia/5226860063/in/photostream/
Copyright 2010

Both are at higher quality and resolution than the ones you linked to, and I've contacted both.

item.title: Re: Actually
item.content:encoded: I did my research, and as far as I can tell, this website is the original source. You might note the fact that this article was written in 2011 and the copyright on the website dates back to 2010. As far as I can tell, that page predates your article and any other source. That said, we're currently trying to ascertain permission from the website to use the pictures.

item.title: Actually
item.content:encoded: The images are pretty much everywhere, but I've found the originals on flickr and contacted them about them being on here.

item.title: Image Removal
item.content:encoded: SCP-112's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Devereaux or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never ridden a rollercoaster and after reading this, I'll probably never want to ride one O.O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is incredible… Probably one of the creepiest things I have ever read on this site, and it was all because of those last "Post-Ride Interviews." The thought of endless psychological torture like that… It's like a train wreck. It's horrifying, but you can't look away.

I wish I could upvote more than once.

item.title: scary
item.content:encoded: Very very scary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great, now I have another reason to never ride a rollercoaster… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked it until I got to the interviews; then I loved it. I am curious, though, what would have happened if one D-Class tried to touch the other during the ride? Like if she tried to shake the dude's arm, or he tried to shake her out of her seemingly hysterical laughter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this :) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of that old 4Chan copypasta MR BONES WILD RIDE!

Anyway, +1 because THE RIDE NEVER ENDS!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I know someone who, after hearing about this, would take a ride on it with a huge backpack of MREs to see if that works.

If it did he'd probaby a) stagger off the coaster and thunderchunder 3 weeks (or more) of MREs into the nearest trashcan before declaring it was awesome or b) declare it was awesome before dying of 3 weeks (or more) worth of MREs in one stomach.

+1. And I hate rollercoasters

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to get off Mr. Bone's Wild Ride!

+1 eeuuugggh

item.title: Re: Squee!
item.content:encoded: Congratulations on your 100th birthday 100th anniversary 100th donut eaten 100th upvote, my good man!

item.title: Re: Squee!
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the +100 club brah.

item.title: Re: Squee!
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations!

:wobbles off the coaster to go puke in a nearby trashcan:

item.title: Squee!
item.content:encoded: You have no idea how happy I am that I've got an article over 100. :unsmith:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bestow the hundredth upvote on thee. Congrats, dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one because of the eerie and completely unsettling way it sort of metaphorically mirrors the greatest fears of a street drug addict. Day after day you're going to buy the ticket and take the ride as Hunter Thompson would put it, and one of those times could very well be that really "bad trip".

*brrrr* Gives me the chills, months on a roller coaster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it upsetting that the guards are not in "Super-Coaster Parks" uniforms. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe literally write it on a piece of paper and upload the image instead of the interview.

OH GOD THIS IS GOOD UPVOTED SO HARD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You two need to re-read the note. It's not a transcription, the guy was writing his responses. If there's stuff like this, the D guy wrote something and then scratched it out. Whoever did the input for the interview, he was obviously told to be as accurate to the original content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Canceled my upvote because of the last transcription. My immersion just got fucked over by the stylistic choices. Everything else is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second interview would be phenomenally better if the transcription at least tried to be grammatically correct. Obviously the D-class isn't going to be using the best grammar after the experience, but the first letter of every sentence should still be capitalized, "daweeMONTHSATATIME" should be "day- wee- MONTHS AT A TIME", etc. It's a transcription made by a paid employee who makes lots of transcriptions, not a post-modernist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interviews definitely add some life to this. Nice job. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me too. It's a gamble, but it could be really sweet. Also, what's up with the last log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll beat everyone to the punch and just admit that I don't need a roller coaster!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I were stuck in a roller coaster for years I'd be pretty obnoxious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually I found the yabbering tone of that last interview so obnoxious and melodramatic that I nearly canceled my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do, do as I did!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Tried to tune her out for daweeMONTHSATATIME…"

Why, oh why, can't I upvote this again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The two interviews are excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Waoh! The intervies was amazing! She had a few minutes ride, she enjoyed it… He on the other hand… Amazing I had to downvote, then upvoted so I could see a change.

item.title: Minor Update 1 of 2OrSo
item.content:encoded:
People took some issue with the history of how the Foundation came across the ride in the first place, so I did a bit of work on that. In real life, I imagine that it's hard for a local gang of thugs to reactivate an amusement park, but otherwise I think it justifies the Foundation getting wind of it. Whatever happened to those on 112 when they first reactivated it, it was enough to turn a Local Gang Does Prank story to Local Gang Does What The Slippery Fuck.

EDIT: Also added two interviews, from two people who rode the train at the same time. Their experiences were not similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Intriguing. Personally I already like it enough for an upvote but would love to see the article expanded further.

On the mildly negative side, the current reason for the Foundation to have taken interest in this object does strike me as rather far-fetched.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this….this is a scary one. I love roller coasters, but jesus, I can one hundred percent relate to how this idea was conceived.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I'd say this does hit the OTT cliche but it does it just right. This is basically a super-duper roller coaster at its heart, the riders are locked in and helpless until the ride is over and all they experience is a roller coaster.

And that's pretty terrifying, it would stop being fun pretty quickly and just keep going and going and going… eight days of torture or more in a three minute ride.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the record, I am working on adding something to the piece. As I'm at work and will be out late, I can't promise it'll be on within the next few hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I agree that the backstory is unrealistic (and completely unnecessary I might add), I have a hard time buying that this hits the OTT cliche.

I would also disagree with regards to the writing; the tone needs work, and some of the diction and phrasing would feel off even in an informal document. No vote, though I may go on a proofreading spree later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote from me, since it is an OTT roller coaster, and yes, cliches can be done well, but the lack of explanation why the foundation determined this particular coaster was anomalous kinda bugs me. It reads like people just reported hating the ride, and the company went out of business, so the foundation bought it to find out why people hated the ride.

The writing is good, except for those couple of nitpicks, so no down vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually sort of want to ride this … .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a phenominal analogy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I just haven't decided yet. I'm not pretending to have voted, is the thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not necessarily. I wouldn't trust what the camera recorded too much. It could have been recording much more, only to have three minutes of it remain once it reached Point Omega.

Analogy time: In photoshop, copy a 600 pixel-wide image. Open a blank 100 pixel-wide image and paste. What do you see? 100 pixels of that 600 pixel image. If you then saved that image as a bitmap file, and someone else loaded it, they'd see a 100-pixel image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a bit of "The Jaunt" in there, but I think you have a good enough twist on it for an upvote.
